  "findings": [
    "Auxiliary heat is locked on during inspection/testing, indicating a control-sequence or thermostat fault rather than normal staging operation.",
    "Condition suggests failed or stuck auxiliary heat relay/contactor, shorted W2/W1 wiring, or thermostat/firmware misconfiguration causing continuous aux heat energization.",
    "Continuous auxiliary heat operation wastes energy, causes excessive supply-air temperatures, and can overload electrical service and heating elements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify thermostat setpoints, differentials, and aux-heat lockout settings against OEM manual.",
    "Inspect and test aux heat relay/contactor and sequencer for welded or stuck contacts; replace if defective.",
    "Check low-voltage wiring between thermostat and heat stages for shorts or incorrect terminations.",
    "Confirm outdoor unit operation and defrost controls; ensure aux heat only energizes on valid demand/call.",
    "Record final amp draw and staging operation after repair; verify aux heat cycles off when not called."
  ],
  "rationale": "Auxiliary heat locked on is an abnormal operating condition that violates normal thermostat/sequence control. A properly operating system should stage aux heat only on demand and de-energize it when the call is satisfied or when outdoor conditions/lockouts permit. The observed condition is an installed-defective control fault requiring correction before the work can be accepted.",
  "references": [
    "OEM service manual - thermostat and control-sequence settings",
    "OEM service manual - auxiliary heat relay/sequencer testing and replacement",
    "OEM service manual - heat staging and lockout parameters"
  ]
}
